title: Index PDF text content
description: Extract and index text from application/pdf response records in WACZ files. Today only text/html is indexed, so archived PDFs are invisible to search. Start with the pure-Rust pdf-extract crate (born-digital PDFs; no OCR).
close_reason: PDF text indexing shipped

title: Add pdf-extract dependency and extract_pdf_text() helper
description: Add the pdf-extract crate and a helper that returns extracted text (and a title if available) from PDF bytes. Pure-Rust, no native deps; born-digital PDFs only (no OCR).
close_reason: PDF text extraction + indexing shipped; 55 tests pass

title: Index application/pdf response records
description: In index.rs, detect response records with mimetype application/pdf and index the extracted text as a page document (URL = the PDF's URL; title from PDF metadata or the URL basename).
close_reason: PDF text extraction + indexing shipped; 55 tests pass

title: Fail gracefully on unparseable PDFs
description: PDF extraction can fail or hang on malformed/encrypted/scanned files. Catch errors, log at debug/warn, and skip the record — never fail the whole index run because of one bad PDF.
close_reason: PDF text extraction + indexing shipped; 55 tests pass

title: PDF fixture and test
description: Add a small WACZ (or WARC) fixture containing a born-digital PDF and assert its text is searchable after indexing.
close_reason: PDF text extraction + indexing shipped; 55 tests pass

title: Index browser-rendered text (urn:text records)
description: Browsertrix stores fully rendered (post-JavaScript) page text as urn:text:<url> WARC records. We currently index only raw text/html, so JS-heavy pages (SPAs like the storymaps fixture) are barely searchable — only their <title>. Index the urn:text payload, mapped back to the real URL, to dramatically improve search over rendered content, with no HTML scraping.
close_reason: urn:text rendered text indexing shipped

title: Detect and parse urn:text: records; map back to real URL
description: In index.rs, recognize response records whose WARC-Target-URI starts with 'urn:text:'. Strip the prefix to recover the real page URL. The payload is plain UTF-8 rendered page text.
close_reason: urn:text rendered text now indexed; per-URL merge; 52 tests pass

title: Restructure indexing to one page doc per URL, best body wins
description: index_warc_for_search currently streams records and indexes each HTML response independently. Restructure to collect per-URL so a page gets a single Tantivy doc: prefer urn:text payload for the body, fall back to HTML-scraped text, and take the title from the HTML <title> when available. Avoid double-indexing the same URL.
close_reason: urn:text rendered text now indexed; per-URL merge; 52 tests pass

title: Tests: rendered SPA text becomes searchable
description: Using a.wacz (storymaps SPA), assert that a distinctive phrase from the rendered body — not just the title — is searchable after indexing. Guards the urn:text path and the before/after search-quality win.
close_reason: urn:text rendered text now indexed; per-URL merge; 52 tests pass

title: browsertrix: import a user's WACZ files from Browsertrix
description:
Add a 'rustyweb browsertrix' subcommand that authenticates to a Browsertrix instance (Webrecorder's hosted crawling service, which produces the WACZ files rustyweb serves), lists a user's archived items, downloads each WACZ into the home directory's archive folder, and indexes it as a local (File) source.

Grounded in the Browsertrix REST API (from its OpenAPI):
- Auth: POST /api/auth/jwt/login (form username/password) -> JWT bearer token.
- Whoami/orgs: GET /api/users/me, GET /api/orgs (paginated).
- List items: GET /api/orgs/{oid}/all-crawls (crawls + uploads), paginated.
- WACZ: GET /api/orgs/{oid}/all-crawls/{id}/download (authenticated stream) for downloading; GET /api/orgs/{oid}/crawls/{id}/replay.json for presigned resource URLs. Collections: /api/orgs/{oid}/collections/{id}/download.

DESIGN
- Command: rustyweb browsertrix [--host URL] [--org SLUG] [--home DIR] [filters].
- Uses --home like the other subcommands (default '.'); WACZs are written under <home>/archive and the index under <home>/index.
- Host default https://app.browsertrix.com; --host for self-hosted.
- Credentials from env (BROWSERTRIX_USER / BROWSERTRIX_PASSWORD) rather than flags, so passwords do not appear in the process list. (Optionally a token.)
- Flow: login -> resolve org (--org or the sole org) -> list all-crawls (paginated) -> for each item, download its WACZ into <home>/archive/<name>.wacz, then index that local file.
- ALWAYS DOWNLOAD (no --no-download for now): stream the authenticated /download endpoint straight into <home>/archive and index as a File source, so it is durable and replayable long-term. This matches rustyweb's rule that local WACZs live under <home>/archive (index stores them home-relative and portable). Browsertrix presigned/replay.json URLs are short-lived, so indexing them as remote URL sources would break replay after expiry - hence we download. (A remote/no-download mode was considered and deferred: rustyweb-15z.4, closed.)
- Provenance + incremental: record the Browsertrix item id on the collection so re-runs skip already-indexed items unless --force.
- Reuse ureq + serde_json; no new heavy deps. Test against a mock HTTP server serving canned JSON, not the live service.

This is a way to close the loop with Webrecorder, whose Browsertrix creates the WACZ files and whose ReplayWeb.page does the replay.

title: Browsertrix API client (auth, orgs, list items, resources)
description: Small module using ureq + serde_json: POST /api/auth/jwt/login for a JWT; GET /api/users/me and /api/orgs to resolve orgs; GET /api/orgs/{oid}/all-crawls (paginated) to list items; helpers to get a WACZ download stream (/all-crawls/{id}/download) and presigned resources (replay.json). Configurable host; bearer auth; handle pagination and API errors.

title: rustyweb browsertrix CLI subcommand + credentials/org selection
description: Add the subcommand with --host (default https://app.browsertrix.com), --org (slug/id; default the sole org), --home (default '.', like the other subcommands; WACZs go under <home>/archive and the index under <home>/index), and basic filters. Always downloads (no --no-download flag for now). Read username/password from env (BROWSERTRIX_USER/BROWSERTRIX_PASSWORD) to keep secrets out of argv; support a token too.

title: Download-and-index flow (always download)
description: For each selected item, stream the authenticated /download endpoint directly into <home>/archive/<name>.wacz (creating archive/ if needed), then index that local file through the existing pipeline (index_path/index_one) so it is registered as a File source stored relative to home. This satisfies rustyweb's rule that local WACZs live under <home>/archive. Choose a stable, filesystem-safe filename from the item (e.g. the crawl/item name or id); set the collection name/metadata from the item. Handle re-runs idempotently (overwrite/skip per the provenance task rustyweb-15z.5).

title: Remote (--no-download) mode via presigned replay.json URLs
description: With --no-download, fetch replay.json and index the presigned WACZ URL(s) as a URL source. Document clearly that Browsertrix presigned URLs are short-lived, so replay stops working after expiry; recommend --download for durable libraries.
close_reason: Deferred: start by always downloading. Presigned Browsertrix URLs are short-lived so the no-download/remote-URL mode is not durable; revisit later if needed.

title: Provenance + incremental sync (skip already-indexed)
description: Record the Browsertrix item id (and instance host) on the Collection (small optional provenance field). On re-run, skip items already indexed unless --force, so syncing a large account is cheap. Consider content hash as a secondary check.

title: Map Browsertrix item metadata to collection metadata
description: Map the item's name/description/created/tags to the collection's name/description/crawl date so imported collections show good metadata on the homepage and collection page.

title: Docs + tests (mock Browsertrix API)
description: Document the browsertrix command (host, org, credentials via env, download vs no-download, expiry caveat) in README/DESIGN. Test the API client and flow against a local HTTP mock serving canned login/orgs/all-crawls JSON and WACZ bytes; do not hit the live service.

title: Portable home directory layout (--home)
description:
Make an archive + its index a single portable folder that can move to another disk, mount point, or machine.

LAYOUT
  <home>/            (default: current directory; --home to override)
  ├── archive/       WACZ files
  └── index/         Tantivy index + collections.json

DESIGN
- One flag --home (default '.') on every subcommand; archive_dir = <home>/archive and index_dir = <home>/index are derived siblings. --home replaces --index-dir as the primary knob (pure --home; overrides can come later if needed).
- Storage: a local WACZ's source is stored RELATIVE to home (e.g. 'archive/docnow.wacz'); that is also what we display. Resolve with home.join(source) to open. Files outside home fall back to an absolute path. Remote http(s) sources still store their URL.
- collection_id derives from the (portable) relative source string, so IDs are stable across moves.
- Downloads (remote URL index, and the coming Browsertrix importer) land in <home>/archive/.
- Back-compat: old manifests with absolute paths still resolve (absolute path used as-is).
- Bonus: 'rustyweb index' with no path indexes everything under <home>/archive/.
- Portability test: index, move the whole home dir, serve/resolve still works.

Reindex is acceptable (single user). Touches CLI (all commands), lib signatures (index/serve/verify/search-url take home and derive/resolve), and collections (relative<->absolute resolution).
close_reason: Portable home-directory layout shipped.

title: Add --home to the CLI; derive archive/ and index/ siblings
description: Add --home (default '.') to index, serve, verify, search-url (and browsertrix later). Derive index_dir=<home>/index, archive_dir=<home>/archive. Replace --index-dir as the primary knob.
close_reason: Implemented: --home layout, relative-to-home storage + resolution, no-arg index, docs + portability test. 67 tests pass.

title: Store WACZ sources relative to home; resolve on open
description: At index time, store a local WACZ's Source::File relative to home when it is under home (e.g. archive/x.wacz), else absolute. Add resolution (home.join(rel)) wherever a File source is opened: serve_file, search_cdx, verify, re-reads. Derive collection_id from the relative string. Old absolute-path manifests still resolve.
close_reason: Implemented: --home layout, relative-to-home storage + resolution, no-arg index, docs + portability test. 67 tests pass.

title: Thread home through lib APIs
description: Update index_location/index_path, server::router, verify, search-url to take the home base (or derived index_dir + archive_dir) and pass it where sources are resolved. Keep the lib testable with explicit dirs.
close_reason: Implemented: --home layout, relative-to-home storage + resolution, no-arg index, docs + portability test. 67 tests pass.

title: Downloads land in <home>/archive/
description: Remote URL indexing (and the Browsertrix importer) write the downloaded WACZ into <home>/archive/ and record it as a relative source, rather than a temp file that is discarded.
close_reason: Deferred: remote-URL index keeps URL semantics by design; download-to-archive belongs to the Browsertrix importer (rustyweb-15z).

title: index with no path indexes <home>/archive/
description: When 'rustyweb index' is run with no path argument, index every .wacz under <home>/archive/.
close_reason: Implemented: --home layout, relative-to-home storage + resolution, no-arg index, docs + portability test. 67 tests pass.

title: Docs + portability test
description: Update README/DESIGN for the home layout. Update existing tests for the new layout. Add a test that indexes, moves the whole home dir to a new path, and confirms serve/resolve still works.
close_reason: Implemented: --home layout, relative-to-home storage + resolution, no-arg index, docs + portability test. 67 tests pass.

title: Searchable page metadata: domain, URL tokens, title boost, AND-by-default + in-app search help
description:
Improve full-text search relevance and expressiveness, and document the query syntax in the app.

Current state: QueryParser has default fields [title, body] only; url/timestamp are STRING (exact-match only) so domain/URL search does not usefully work; multi-word queries are OR by default; no in-app help.

Scope (this slice):
- Schema: add 'domain' (STRING|STORED, exact host for domain: filter + display) and 'url_tokens' (TEXT, tokenized URL words) to the Tantivy schema.
- Extraction: derive host + tokenized URL text from each page URL (url crate).
- Query: default fields become [title, body, url_tokens]; set_conjunction_by_default() (AND); boost title over body; use lenient parsing so malformed queries do not 500.
- Docs: in-app 'Search tips' disclosure on homepage + results page documenting the real working syntax (phrases, field search title:/domain:, +/-, OR, grouping, boost).
- Update DESIGN.md schema table + README search note.
- Tests for domain extraction, url tokens, domain: filtering, AND semantics.

Note: adding schema fields requires re-indexing existing collections (idempotent upsert).

Deferred follow-ups: crawl-date as DATE/numeric range field, meta_description/OpenGraph, headings, lang/mime filters.
close_reason: Implemented: domain + url_tokens fields, AND-by-default, title boost, lenient parsing, in-app Search tips, DESIGN/README updates. Tests green.

title: Richer page metadata for search (phase 2)
description: Follow-ups to rustyweb-search-metadata-h6k: index more per-page metadata to improve relevance and enable filtering. Each is a schema addition (requires re-indexing) plus extraction, query wiring, tips/docs, and tests.
close_reason: All phase-2 metadata tasks done: description+headings, year, type+lang.

title: HTML metadata: meta description + headings
description: Extract <meta name=description>/og:description and <h1>/<h2> headings during HTML text extraction. Add 'description' (TEXT|STORED) and 'headings' (TEXT) fields; include in default query fields with headings boosted between title and body. Expose description in the API and use as a snippet fallback. Refactor index_page to take a Page struct so future fields don't churn the signature.
close_reason: Implemented: HtmlText struct (title/body/description/headings), Page struct for index_page, description+headings fields searchable with headings boost, description snippet fallback + API. Tests green.

title: Crawl year filtering (numeric range field)
description: Add a numeric 'year' field derived from the 14-digit page timestamp, enabling year:2021 and range year:[2020 TO 2023] filtering.
close_reason: Implemented: numeric year field from timestamp; year:2021 exact + year:[a TO b] range filtering. Tests green.

title: Media type and language filters
description: Add 'type' (html/pdf) and 'lang' (from <html lang>) STRING fields for type:pdf and lang:en filtering.
close_reason: Implemented: type (html/pdf) and lang (primary subtag) STRING fields; type:pdf and lang:en filtering. Tests green.

title: Schema-migration guard + reindex subcommand
description:
Two related changes so schema additions don't panic and can be migrated cleanly:

1. Schema guard: when opening an existing Tantivy index whose stored schema differs from the current build_schema(), fail with a clear error telling the user to run 'rustyweb reindex' instead of panicking on a missing field.

2. 'rustyweb reindex' subcommand: rebuild the full-text index from the sources already in collections.json (preserving the manifest and each collection's name), re-fetching remote URL sources too, and recreating the Tantivy index from scratch so a new schema is picked up. Missing local files are skipped with a warning.

Update DESIGN.md CLI section and README.
close_reason: Implemented: schema guard in SearchIndex::open (clear 'run reindex' error instead of panic on stale schema) + 'rustyweb reindex' subcommand rebuilding from collections.json (re-fetches URLs, preserves names, skips missing files). Tests + docs done.

title: Require explicit paths/URLs for index (drop archive auto-scan)
description: Now that reindex rebuilds from collections.json (and re-fetches URL sources), the bare 'index' archive-directory auto-scan is a redundant half-feature (it only ever covered local files). Make 'index' require one or more paths/URLs; a directory arg still scans for .wacz, so 'rustyweb index archive/' replaces bare 'index'. Emit a helpful error on no args pointing to both 'index archive/' and 'reindex'. Update README + DESIGN.
close_reason: index now requires >=1 path/URL; bare index prints guidance (index archive/ or reindex) and exits 2. Directory scanning retained. Docs updated.

title: index: drop directory support; require local WACZs to live in <home>/archive
description: index now accepts only .wacz files and http(s) URLs (no directory scanning). A local WACZ must already live under <home>/archive; rustyweb indexes it in place (does NOT copy) and stores a home-relative source, so the home dir stays self-contained/portable. A path outside archive, a directory, or a non-.wacz file is an error with guidance; several at once via shell glob (index archive/*.wacz). Remote URLs are exempt. Enforced in the lib (resolve_sources) per the thin-bin/all-logic-in-lib design. Tests updated to stage fixtures under archive/; new tests for outside-archive and directory rejection and relative-source storage.
close_reason: Implemented in resolve_sources with clear errors; docs (README/DESIGN/CLI help/bare-index guidance) updated; tests green (60 lib + 30 integration).

title: Search enrichment inspired by SolrWayback / warc-indexer
description:
Index more per-record and HTML signals as queryable fields to improve archive search, inspired by SolrWayback and the British Library warc-indexer (which runs Tika/DROID and derives a large Solr schema). rustyweb adopts only lightweight, pure-Rust pieces that fit the single-binary, no-heavy-deps philosophy; full-text search over HTML/PDF/rendered-text stays the core and is unaffected.

Each new field is a schema addition, so it requires a rebuild via rustyweb reindex (the schema guard already errors and points there). Extraction lives in extract_html_text / collect_page_records; schema in build_schema; query wiring in SearchIndex::search.

In scope (child tasks):
- meta keywords + author (HTML metadata)
- content-based language detection fallback (whatlang)
- registrable-domain (public suffix) for a site: filter spanning subdomains
- outbound link host/domain fields (link-based discovery / future link graph)
- last-modified date facet

Deferred / out of scope for now (keep it minimal): image enrichment (dimensions, EXIF geo, perceptual/PDQ image search), entity/geo extraction (locations, postcodes), fuzzy near-duplicate hashing (ssdeep), elements-used analytics, and Tika-style text extraction from arbitrary binary formats (needs a heavy extractor). Open question also deferred: whether to index non-text resources (images/js/css) as stub docs to power a real media-type facet.

Refs: https://github.com/netarchivesuite/solrwayback and warc-indexer SolrFields.java in https://github.com/ukwa/webarchive-discovery

title: Index HTML meta keywords and author
description: Extract meta keywords (meta[name=keywords]) and author (meta[name=author], with og/article fallbacks) during HTML extraction and index them. Add keywords (TEXT, searchable via the default query fields) and author (TEXT, searchable and usable as author:name) to the schema; populate via HtmlText and the Page struct, mirroring the existing description/headings work. Mirrors warc-indexer keywords/author. Low effort. Requires reindex.

title: Content-based language detection fallback
description: When a page has no html lang attribute, detect the language from the extracted body text with a lightweight pure-Rust detector (whatlang) and store its primary subtag in the existing lang field. Keep the html lang attribute as the primary source; only fall back when it is absent or empty. Improves coverage of the lang: filter. Mirrors warc-indexer content_language (which uses Tika). Adds the whatlang dependency (small). Requires reindex.

title: Registrable-domain field for a cross-subdomain site: filter
description: Add a registrable-domain field (the private domain: one label above the public suffix, via the public suffix list / psl crate) so a site: filter matches a whole site across subdomains and handles multi-level suffixes like example.co.uk. Today domain holds the raw host (www.example.com), so subdomains and the bare domain do not unify. Keep domain (host) for exact host filtering and add site (registrable domain) for cross-subdomain filtering; both derived from the page URL. Mirrors the warc-indexer host / domain / public_suffix split. Adds the psl dependency (bundled public suffix list) - confirm it fits the single-binary goal. Highest search-quality win for archives with many subdomains. Requires reindex.

title: Index outbound link hosts and domains
description: Extract outbound hyperlink targets (a[href], resolved against the page URL) and index their hosts and domains as multi-valued fields (links_hosts, links_domains), enabling queries like links_hosts:youtube.com (pages that link to a host) and a future link-graph view. Mirrors warc-indexer links_hosts / links_domains / links_images. Extraction is easy with scraper; note the index-size cost of storing link targets. Decide whether to also capture links_images. Requires reindex.

title: Last-Modified date facet
description: Capture the HTTP Last-Modified response header (response records already parse http_headers) as a second date signal: store last_modified and/or last_modified_year for filtering, complementing crawl year. Mirrors warc-indexer last_modified / last_modified_year. Small; helps distinguish when content was authored vs when it was crawled. Requires reindex.
